mnemonics.title: DFS Traversal Order
mnemonics.content: D-I-S-C-O-V-E-R: Discover (GRAY) → In-stack → Search neighbors → Complete (BLACK) → Output (reverse) → Verify parent → Explore Recursively. Helps remember: discovery time, processing, backtrack, finish time, output order.

mnemonics.title: Edge Classification
mnemonics.content: T-B-F-C: Tree (first visit), Back (to ancestor), Forward (to descendant), Cross (neither). Remember: 'Tree Begins From Child; Back Goes to Parent; Forward Goes Forward; Cross Cuts Across'.

mnemonics.title: Topological Sort via DFS
mnemonics.content: Reverse Finish Times = Topo Order. Think: 'Finish First, Print Last' - the last vertex to finish is source of all others.

acronyms.usage: Explores as deep as possible before backtracking, using LIFO principle
Timestamps marking when vertex u is first visited and when exploration completes
Maximal set where every vertex reachable from every other; found via Kosaraju's algorithm using DFS

acronyms.expansion: Vertex currently being processed (on recursion stack)
acronyms.usage: Indicates vertex is ancestor; back edge to GRAY indicates cycle
Tree edge created when exploring edge to WHITE vertex

visualTips.visualization: Imagine DFS as diving deep into a cave: go as far as possible along one tunnel (branch), mark all reachable points, then backtrack to last junction and try next tunnel. Each discovery creates a parent-child link in tree.

Draw discovery (d) and finish (f) as brackets around each vertex's interval. Nested intervals = ancestor relationship. Disjoint intervals = no ancestor relationship. Visual: [A [B] [C]] shows A ancestor of B and C.

Draw DFS tree vertically with time arrows. Tree edge: down to new node. Back edge: up to ancestor. Forward edge: down to already-visited descendant. Cross edge: horizontal or to subtree already finished.

analogies.analogy: Exploring a maze: go straight as far as possible, when hit dead-end, backtrack to last intersection and try new path. Like a mouse exploring tunnels—always goes deeper before exploring alternatives.
Like a stack of plates: last plate pushed is first popped. DFS pushes vertices when discovered, pops when finished. Current path of exploration is like plates waiting to be processed.

Finding a loop in path: if while walking you encounter someone you met earlier in current walk, you've found a loop. GRAY = met earlier in current walk; BLACK = already finished, no longer in current path.
Task dependencies: if task A must complete before B, finish time of A > finish time of B. So doing tasks in reverse finish time ensures prerequisites completed first.
